cases.title: OpenAI Board Crisis
cases.summary: Sam Altman was removed from OpenAI, employee backlash escalated immediately, Microsoft moved to absorb key staff, and Altman returned within days with a restructured board.
cases.ground_truth_summary: The dominant realized path was a fast re-consolidation around management continuity rather than a durable governance reset or prolonged organizational fracture.
cases.suggested_simulation_requirement: Predict the near-term employee, partner, and market reaction to a sudden AI-lab leadership removal and the likely stabilized outcome over the next 2 weeks.

cases.title: Silicon Valley Bank Collapse
cases.summary: Silicon Valley Bank failed after a concentrated depositor run, venture-backed companies scrambled for payroll safety, and regulators moved rapidly to contain broader panic.
cases.ground_truth_summary: The realized path was a sharp liquidity shock followed by fast policy containment, not a full systemic banking meltdown but not a clean no-contagion outcome either.
cases.suggested_simulation_requirement: Predict the immediate market, startup, and regulatory reaction to a sudden regional-bank liquidity crisis and the likely outcome over the next month.

cases.title: CrowdStrike Global Outage
cases.summary: A faulty CrowdStrike update caused widespread Windows failures across airlines, hospitals, banks, and enterprises, triggering a global operational shock.
cases.ground_truth_summary: The realized path was an acute operational crisis with broad reputational damage and customer scrutiny, but not a permanent category collapse for endpoint security.
cases.suggested_simulation_requirement: Predict the near-term customer, regulator, and market reaction to a global software update outage affecting critical infrastructure and large enterprises.

cases.title: FTX Collapse
cases.summary: FTX collapsed after a confidence crisis around reserves and related-party exposure, causing rapid counterparty panic, legal fallout, and sector-wide distrust.
cases.ground_truth_summary: The realized path was a severe trust collapse with extended regulatory and reputational consequences, not a quick contained exchange failure.
cases.suggested_simulation_requirement: Predict the near-term customer, regulatory, and market reaction to a major crypto exchange insolvency and the likely sector-level fallout over the next quarter.

cases.title: Meta Threads Launch
cases.summary: Meta launched Threads into a moment of instability at Twitter/X, triggering rapid signup growth, strong media attention, and questions about long-term retention.
cases.ground_truth_summary: The realized path was a strong launch spike followed by a retention and engagement normalization phase rather than immediate decisive platform replacement.
cases.suggested_simulation_requirement: Predict the near-term user, creator, advertiser, and media reaction to a new social platform launching into a competitor trust crisis.
